title: Loss aversion
script_summary: Explore the intriguing concept of loss aversion and its implications in decision-making.

segments.voiceover: What if losing $100 feels worse than gaining $200?
This is loss aversion, a phenomenon where losses weigh heavier on our minds than equivalent gains.
Studies show we feel pain from loss about 2.5 times more intensely than joy from a gain.
This plays a crucial role in our decisions, often leading to risk-averse behavior.
Understanding this can help you evaluate risks more rationally and make better choices.
Curious about more mind science? Follow for insights!
